task_metadata.current_task: In __exit__, calculate elapsed time and accumulate it in the class-level timings dictionary under the label
task_metadata.status: Implementing context manager exit and timing accumulation logic

nodes.description: Design context manager class structure for timing code blocks with per-label summary reporting
nodes.rationale: A context manager is the most Pythonic way to handle timing code blocks, using __enter__ and __exit__ methods. We need to support multiple labels to track different code sections, and accumulate timing data for per-label summaries.
nodes.task_state: Starting implementation of context manager for code block timing

nodes.description: Use class-level dictionary to store accumulated timing data indexed by label, and instance-level tracking of current block start time
nodes.rationale: Class-level storage allows summaries to persist across multiple context manager uses. Instance-level start_time tracks the current block being timed. This enables the same label to be used multiple times and accumulate durations.
nodes.task_state: Designing data structures for timing accumulation and summary reporting

nodes.description: Implement __init__ method that accepts a label parameter and records start time in __enter__
nodes.rationale: The label identifies which code block is being timed, and capturing the start time in __enter__ ensures we measure from the exact point the context is entered, before user code runs.
nodes.task_state: Implementing context manager entry and initialization

nodes.description: In __exit__, calculate elapsed time and accumulate it in the class-level timings dictionary under the label
nodes.rationale: __exit__ runs after the user code completes, so we can calculate the actual elapsed time. Accumulating rather than replacing allows multiple uses of the same label to contribute to a total.
nodes.task_state: Implementing context manager exit and timing accumulation logic

edges.rationale: Data structure design decisions follow from the overall context manager design pattern. The storage approach implements the architecture established in node_001.
Exit logic follows from entry logic. The __exit__ method must work with data set up in __init__ and __enter__ to calculate and record elapsed time.
